data.body:
Scope expansion (2026-04-26, from docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md):

Two mitigations absorbed into this epic's atomicity-check scope:

- **G6-M3** — Generalize the operational-atomicity check to also cover known-antipattern audits at brainstorm/preplanning time. When an epic touches a file class associated with a known antipattern (shell hooks → CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, runners → semantic drift, etc.), inject a pre-emptive task: 'Audit for <antipattern> across all files in scope before implementation begins.' The known-antipattern registry is delivered by New Epic A (static-analysis defense layer) per docs/new-epics-4-26.md; this epic consumes the registry at planning time.
- **G11-M2** — Implementation-plan templated paired sub-task: when a task adds a config key in dso-config.conf or any *.conf, mandatorily emit a paired sub-task documenting the key in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (description, type, default, scope, example). Same atomicity pattern as the artifact-desync case (release.sh + marketplace.json that motivated this epic): two artifacts must update together.

Rationale: this epic's existing scope is the artifact-reference desync pattern (Script A creates artifact X, file Y references X but is not updated atomically). Both additions extend the same mechanism to additional artifact pairs (code + antipattern audit; config-key + reference doc). The detection-injection point (red-team-reviewer or AC generation) is the same.

This epic has no children yet (scrutiny:pending); decomposition will pick up the new SCs naturally. Note: tag scrutiny:pending should be cleared once brainstorm runs on this epic.
